title: R1' Agent-facing MCP Contract (C1 SSoT)
description: Single source of truth for every tool the coding-agent calls. Providers: cks (code-knowledge-system), chainbench, jira-gateway. ckv/ckg are dev-only and intentionally NOT in this contract. Each provider's conformance test validates its registered tools against this file; coding-agent's contract/lint-tool-names.sh asserts every mcp__server__tool reference in the agent/command prompts is a tool name listed here.

cks_context_get_for_task.description: Compose a budgeted evidence pack for a development task (the primary planning entrypoint): intent -> semantic -> keyword/graph -> budget -> sanitize.

cks_context_semantic_search.description: Vector (meaning) search over the indexed corpus. Hits carry source=ckv.

cks_context_search_text.description: BM25 keyword (exactness) search over the indexed corpus. Hits carry source=ckg.

cks_context_find_symbol.description: Resolve a symbol name to its definition citation(s).

cks_context_find_callers.description: List the callers of a symbol (reverse call edges).

cks_context_find_callees.description: List the callees of a symbol (forward call edges).

cks_context_get_subgraph.description: Return all nodes/edges within depth hops of a symbol, all edge types.

cks_context_impact_analysis.description: Reverse-dependency closure of a symbol, grouped by coupling category (callers/interface/type_users/distributed/concurrent/other).

cks_context_concurrency_impact.description: Concurrency blast radius of a symbol: goroutines/channels/locks it spawns/sends-to/acquires and modules reached over concurrency edges, both directions.

cks_context_change_history.description: PR refs (provenance) and/or BM25-ranked hunk evidence for a symbol or intent.

cks_ops_health.description: Backend reachability + version pins. Reports degraded when ckv (embedder) is unavailable.

cks_ops_freshness.description: Index-vs-HEAD staleness: indexed head, current head, changed files.

cks_ops_index.description: Refresh the ckv + ckg indexes (maintenance op). mode=incremental|full.

chainbench_init.description: Initialize a local go-stablenet network from a profile (genesis + TOML + datadirs).

chainbench_start.description: Start all chain nodes of an initialized network.

chainbench_status.description: Per-node block height, peers, running state, and consensus health.

chainbench_test_run.description: Run a built-in test by 'category/name' (e.g. basic/consensus, basic/tx-send).

chainbench_report.description: Test results report. With format=json returns the C4 loop-back shape parsed for summary.failed.

chainbench_failure_context.description: Most recent failure context snapshot (per-node height, peers, syncing, log tails).

chainbench_stop.description: Stop all running chain nodes gracefully (local network only).

chainbench_restart.description: Restart the chain: stop all nodes, clean data, re-initialize with the same profile, and start fresh.

chainbench_state_compact.description: Compact JSON snapshot (<300B) of current chain state: running status, profile, per-node height/peers, consensus health, last test result.

chainbench_test_list.description: List all available built-in tests, organized by category (e.g. basic/consensus, basic/tx-send).

chainbench_contract_deploy.description: Deploy a contract by signing a contract-creation tx (to: nil). mode 'legacy' (gas_price) or '1559' (max_fee + max_priority). Returns {tx_hash, contract_address}; confirm via chainbench_tx_wait.

chainbench_contract_call.description: Read-only contract call (eth_call). Provide raw calldata, or abi+method+args for server-side encoding. Returns result_raw always; result_decoded only in ABI mode.

chainbench_tx_send.description: Send a signed transaction. Modes: 'legacy' (gas_price), '1559' (max_fee + max_priority), 'set_code' (EIP-7702, authorization_list), 'fee_delegation' (go-stablenet 0x16, fee_payer). Signer/fee_payer are aliases; key material never crosses the MCP boundary.

chainbench_tx_wait.description: Poll for a tx receipt with exponential backoff; returns receipt fields when mined (status, block_number, gas_used, logs_count, ...) or {status:'pending'} on timeout. Default timeout_ms 60000.

chainbench_txpool_inspect.description: Transaction pool status (pending/queued counts) across all running nodes or a specific node.

chainbench_node_rpc.description: Send a JSON-RPC call directly to a specific node and return the response (e.g. eth_blockNumber, eth_getBalance, net_peerCount).

chainbench_consensus_status.description: WBFT consensus activity statistics over a block range: validator participation, block-authoring distribution, round stability, seal signature counts.

chainbench_consensus_health.description: Quick consensus health check across all running nodes: block production, sync status, validator agreement, round stability.

chainbench_consensus_validators.description: WBFT validator set at a specific block (validator addresses and count). Omit block for latest.

chainbench_consensus_block_info.description: Decoded WBFT extra data from a block header: consensus round, prepared/committed seals, RANDAO reveal, gas tip, epoch info.

chainbench_network_partition.description: Create or heal a network partition between two node groups (admin_removePeer / admin_addPeer) to test BFT consensus under splits.

chainbench_network_peers.description: Peer connectivity for all running nodes or a specific node: peer count, connected peer IDs, topology overview.

chainbench_network_topology.description: Set a P2P topology for testing: 'full-mesh', 'hub-spoke' (via hub node), or 'ring'. Useful for testing consensus under restricted connectivity.

chainbench_log_search.description: Search node log files for a grep-compatible regex pattern; returns matching log lines. Omit node to search all nodes.

chainbench_log_timeline.description: Consensus event timeline from all node logs: block proposals, votes, commits, and view changes ordered by timestamp.

chainbench_events_get.description: Fetch event logs (eth_getLogs) with optional address / block-range / topics filtering. When abi + event are both given, each log is decoded server-side as decoded:{event,args}.

jira_read_ticket.description: Read a Jira ticket. Returns fields + _filter_metadata.scan_result (CLEAN/REDACTED/BLOCKED).

jira_add_comment.description: Add a comment to a ticket.
